questions.question: Why isn't one attention head enough?
options: One head is numerically unstable

A single head produces ONE attention matrix capturing one kind of relationship — subject-verb agreement, co-reference, discourse, and chunking get smeared into one softmax distribution and half the signal is lost
Single heads can't be trained by SGD
One head can only see 64 tokens
questions.explanation: The Vaswani fix: run several attention functions in parallel with separate Q/K/V projections, each in a d_model/n_heads subspace — same total parameters, more expressive power.

questions.question: What is the split-attend-concat mechanic?
options: Splitting the batch across GPUs

Project X to Q/K/V, reshape (N, d_model) into (n_heads, N, d_head), run scaled dot-product attention independently per head, stack back to (N, d_model), and multiply by the learned output matrix W_o — which is where heads finally get to mix
Splitting the sequence into chunks
Running the same head several times
questions.explanation: Heads never talk during the attention computation itself — they specialize in disjoint subspaces, and W_o recombines their findings.

questions.question: What head specializations have probing studies found?
options: All heads learn identical patterns
Positional heads, previous-token heads, copy heads, named-entity heads, and induction heads — the last underlying in-context learning itself
Heads only encode punctuation
Specialization is a debunked myth
questions.explanation: Specialization without competition is why heads work: each gets its own representational budget instead of fighting within one softmax.

questions.question: What problem do MQA and GQA solve, and how?
options: Training instability at scale

KV-cache memory at inference: multi-query shares ONE K/V head across all query heads, grouped-query uses G groups (e.g. N/8) — GQA cuts KV-cache by N/G with nearly full quality and is the modern default (Llama 3, Qwen 2+, Mistral)
Vanishing gradients in deep stacks
Tokenizer vocabulary explosion
questions.explanation: The variant table is an inference-economics story: full MHA (GPT-2, BERT) pays maximum cache; the lineage trades K/V diversity for serving memory.

questions.question: How does Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA) go further than GQA?
options: It removes attention entirely
It compresses K/V into a low-rank latent space and projects back at compute time — spending extra FLOPs to save much more memory (DeepSeek-V2/V3)
It quantizes the cache to 1 bit
It caches only the first token
questions.explanation: The same trade pushed to its limit: FLOPs are cheap and growing cheaper, HBM is the scarce resource — so recompute what you'd otherwise store.
